# 34 Vendor Research Evidence Register

        This register records the official sources used during seq_034. Retrieval date is `2026-04-10` for every row unless a later recrawl is needed.

        | Evidence ID | Vendor | Type | Captured on | Link | Summary |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ev_dg_callback | Deepgram | webhook | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://developers.deepgram.com/docs/using-callbacks-to-return-transcripts-to-your-server) | Deepgram supports asynchronous transcription callbacks to a URL supplied with the transcription request. |
| ev_dg_prerecorded | Deepgram | async_jobs | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://developers.deepgram.com/docs/pre-recorded-audio) | Deepgram exposes pre-recorded transcription flows that can run synchronously or via callback for longer files. |
| ev_dg_interim | Deepgram | partial_results | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://developers.deepgram.com/docs/interim-results) | Deepgram streaming supports interim results so partial transcript states can remain explicit before finalization. |
| ev_dg_projects | Deepgram | project_model | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://developers.deepgram.com/docs/managing-projects) | Deepgram separates usage and access through projects, which aligns with environment-scoped provisioning later on. |
| ev_dg_api_keys | Deepgram | credentials | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://developers.deepgram.com/docs/create-additional-api-keys) | Deepgram supports additional API keys, which helps isolate preview and live environments later. |
| ev_dg_deployment | Deepgram | residency | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://developers.deepgram.com/docs/deployment-options) | Deepgram documents hosted, self-hosted, and private deployment paths, which keeps residency and portability options open. |
| ev_dg_pricing | Deepgram | pricing | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://deepgram.com/pricing) | Deepgram publishes usage-based pricing tiers, making spend controls explicit for later gated onboarding. |
| ev_aa_webhooks_prerecorded | AssemblyAI | webhook | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://www.assemblyai.com/docs/deployment/webhooks) | AssemblyAI lets clients attach webhook URLs to transcript jobs and retries failed deliveries up to ten attempts. |
| ev_aa_webhooks_streaming | AssemblyAI | streaming | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://www.assemblyai.com/docs/deployment/webhooks-for-streaming-speech-to-text) | AssemblyAI exposes webhook delivery for streaming jobs and keeps incremental transcript events explicit. |
| ev_aa_region | AssemblyAI | residency | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://www.assemblyai.com/docs/pre-recorded-audio/select-the-region) | AssemblyAI offers explicit region selection with US and EU endpoints for pre-recorded transcription workloads. |
| ev_aa_delete | AssemblyAI | deletion | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://www.assemblyai.com/docs/api-reference/transcripts/delete) | AssemblyAI documents transcript deletion and states that deleting a transcript also removes its uploaded files. |
| ev_aa_private_deployment | AssemblyAI | deployment | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://www.assemblyai.com/docs/deployment/private-deployment) | AssemblyAI documents private deployment options, including VPC and on-prem paths, which helps portability and residency review. |
| ev_aa_pricing | AssemblyAI | pricing | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://www.assemblyai.com/pricing) | AssemblyAI publishes usage pricing and enterprise deployment options, which is enough to model later spend gates. |
| ev_azure_batch_create | Azure AI Speech | batch_jobs | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/speech-service/batch-transcription-create) | Azure batch transcription supports job creation, customer storage integration, and webhook registration with validation handshake rules. |
| ev_azure_batch_overview | Azure AI Speech | retention | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/speech-service/batch-transcription) | Azure batch transcription documents job lifecycle, content URLs, and TTL behavior that affects later retention policy design. |
| ev_azure_privacy | Azure AI Speech | privacy | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/legal/cognitive-services/speech-service/speech-to-text/data-privacy-security) | Microsoft documents privacy, logging, and container posture for speech-to-text workloads, which is useful but operationally heavy. |
| ev_aws_transcribe_partial | Amazon Transcribe | partial_results | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/transcribe/latest/dg/streaming-partial-results.html) | Amazon Transcribe exposes partial-result stabilization for streaming transcription, which aligns with explicit partial versus final states. |
| ev_aws_transcribe_io | Amazon Transcribe | retention | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/transcribe/latest/dg/how-input.html) | Amazon Transcribe couples batch jobs to S3 input and output paths, which is strong for audit but increases bucket and KMS coupling. |
| ev_google_overview | Google Cloud Speech-to-Text | overview | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://cloud.google.com/speech-to-text) | Google positions Speech-to-Text as a Cloud product but the product page also carries a caution not to enter sensitive, confidential, or personal information. |
| ev_google_endpoints | Google Cloud Speech-to-Text | residency | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://cloud.google.com/speech-to-text/docs/endpoints) | Google documents global and regional endpoints, which helps environment separation but still leaves healthcare contract clarity to later review. |
| ev_google_pricing | Google Cloud Speech-to-Text | pricing | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://cloud.google.com/speech-to-text/pricing) | Google publishes pricing and batch versus streaming tiers, but the product posture is not strong enough here to offset the PHI caution. |
| ev_guardduty_enable | GuardDuty Malware Protection for S3 | project_model | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/guardduty/latest/ug/enable-malware-protection-s3-bucket.html) | AWS documents enabling a protection plan per bucket scope, service role, EventBridge rule, and tagging permissions. |
| ev_guardduty_how | GuardDuty Malware Protection for S3 | quarantine_states | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/guardduty/latest/ug/how-malware-protection-for-s3-gdu-works.html) | AWS describes status values, object tagging, and scan outcomes that map well to clean, suspicious, failed, and unsupported states. |
| ev_guardduty_monitoring | GuardDuty Malware Protection for S3 | observability | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/guardduty/latest/ug/monitoring-malware-protection-s3-scans-gdu.html) | AWS exposes scan monitoring through tags, CloudWatch metrics, findings, and EventBridge, which is strong for audit and quarantine hooks. |
| ev_guardduty_eventbridge | GuardDuty Malware Protection for S3 | webhook | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/guardduty/latest/ug/monitor-with-eventbridge-s3-malware-protection.html) | GuardDuty emits EventBridge events for scan lifecycle changes, which fits a replay-safe internal event-ingress pattern better than raw vendor webhooks. |
| ev_guardduty_pricing | GuardDuty Malware Protection for S3 | pricing | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/guardduty/latest/ug/pricing-malware-protection-for-s3-guardduty.html) | AWS publishes per-object and per-GB pricing, which makes spend governance explicit enough for later gating. |
| ev_opswat_product | OPSWAT MetaDefender Cloud | overview | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://www.opswat.com/products/metadefender/cloud) | OPSWAT positions MetaDefender Cloud as a multi-engine scanning service with hash lookup, CDR, and file-analysis workflows. |
| ev_opswat_api | OPSWAT MetaDefender Cloud | api | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://www.opswat.com/docs/mdcloud/integrations/v4-api) | OPSWAT documents REST API flows, API key authentication, hash lookup, and file submission patterns for cloud scanning. |
| ev_opswat_regions | OPSWAT MetaDefender Cloud | residency | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://www.opswat.com/docs/my/2025.1.3/home/cloud-services) | OPSWAT documents cloud-service regions and service boundaries, which gives it a workable residency and hosting story. |
| ev_cloudmersive_product | Cloudmersive Virus Scan API | overview | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://cloudmersive.com/virus-api) | Cloudmersive markets a file and cloud-storage scanning API with cloud-hosted, dedicated, and on-premise deployment options. |
| ev_cloudmersive_api | Cloudmersive Virus Scan API | api | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://api.cloudmersive.com/docs/virus.asp) | Cloudmersive documents API-key authentication and scan endpoints for files, archives, and S3-hosted objects. |
| ev_cloudmersive_pricing | Cloudmersive Virus Scan API | pricing | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://cloudmersive.com/pricing) | Cloudmersive publishes hosted, dedicated, and private deployment pricing levers, which helps later spend control review. |
| ev_vt_private_scanning | VirusTotal Private Scanning | overview | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://docs.virustotal.com/docs/private-scanning) | VirusTotal positions private scanning as isolated from the public corpus, but the product still remains threat-intel-centric rather than quarantine-workflow-centric. |
| ev_vt_files_api | VirusTotal Private Scanning | api | 2026-04-10 | [source](https://docs.virustotal.com/reference/files) | VirusTotal documents file upload and analysis APIs, but the workflow is still less aligned to private quarantine-state promotion than the stronger shortlist options. |
